ref: All at once Moses and Elijah were there talking with Jesus.
text: *Elii n a hɩnka ŋ nɩ kan Moyiisi kɩ, ŋn'ɩ meer bam kan Yeezuu kɩ.
translated: He is in the word of Moses, and he comes to the word of Jesus.

ref: Then Jesus told the man, “Hold out your hand.” The man did, and it became as healthy as the other one.
text: A m bɩ, an a hɩ wɔgərzaa bɩ m, a ʊ: «Ɩ wɔ bɩ hʊr!» Gʊaa bɩ n a hʊr, a wɔ bɩ n a banka mɩŋŋa am deem bɩ hɔ bɩ m.
translated: He said to me, and he told me, saying: "He has come!" When he arrived, he told me that he had come from the white bank and he was looking for me.

ref: When Jesus got up the next morning, he was hungry. He started out for the city,
text: Yeezuu k'a wusigə dɔgta mɩŋŋa m, an nɩ tam kʊnɔ ʊ b'ʊ bɩ, nɔ a nyasʊ.
translated: Jesus said, "My name, and you have come to know the Father through me, so that they may believe."

ref: If a soldier forces you to carry his pack one kilometer, carry it two kilometers.
text: Kʊ gʊaa paŋŋa ba ɩ ma, k'a ɩ da tarɛ ma ganjɩbɔʊrɛ tisi kan a kɩ, k'ɩ ta ganjɩbɔʊrɛ tisi hɩɩya kan kɩ.
translated: And he went to the place where he was; when he finished his work, he went home, and he did the work well at home.

ref: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
text: Zaan bɩ, yoonde ka huu a du a ma, an kʊmbar so a ʊ. Bɩsɩnɔ kan zɔɔn kɩ a n'a bɩrɛ.
translated: They said, you did it to them, and they will come to you. Bisina went to go if they do not return.

ref: Night and day he was in the graveyard or on the hills, yelling and cutting himself with stones.
text: Sinuu ʊ kan a gunuu ʊ kɩ, gəənoyaarɔ an nɩ wʊtaŋ ʊ kan ciro wɔɔ burə kɩ, zee an n'a dam, an nɩ yaaba darɛ a mɩŋŋa ma jaarɔ m.
translated: When Sinuu had no food or was hungry, they went to the river at Gəənoyaarɔ to cross where there is a big tree, then they could not speak the language, so they needed help to understand.

ref: The seeds that fell among the thornbushes are also people who hear the message. But they start worrying about the needs of this life and are fooled by the desire to get rich. So the message gets choked out, and they never produce anything.
text: Hɔyaa k'a nɩ laarɔ bireyaa ʊ bɩ, gʊɔɔ m, ŋnɩ Woso lemim bɩ ma, durnya hɔɔndama kan a arzaka lɛtaama kɩ ŋ lemim bɩ zɛ, a bɩr nyɩ darɛ y.

ref: While Jesus was still speaking to the crowds, his mother and brothers came and stood outside because they wanted to talk with him.
text: Yeezuu k'a nɩ meer bam kan gʊɔɔ cir bɩ kɩ bɩ, a da kan a danyɩnɔ kɩ bʊr, ŋnɩ jɩm garga ra, ŋn'a ŋʊam k'a meer ba kan kɩ. [
translated: For God so loved the world that he gave his one and only Son, so that everyone who believes in him may not perish but have eternal life.

ref: Someone told Jesus, “Your mother and brothers are standing outside and want to talk with you.”
text: Gʊaa n a hɩ Yeezuu m, a ʊ: «Ɩ da kan ɩ danyɩnɔ kɩ ɩ garga ra, ŋ lɛ n taa k'a meer ba kan ɩ kɩ.»]
translated: And Jesus said, "I have come to gather the lost ones, not to abandon them."

ref: It was taken on a serving plate to the girl, and she gave it to her mother.
text: an bʊr m laaga ʊ, an b'a ka nyɩmbʊɛɛrɛ b'ʊ, an a si, an aa a ka a da ʊ.
translated: I went there, I saw something, I did, I did it again.

ref: Jesus knew what they were thinking, so he said to them: Any kingdom where people fight each other will end up ruined. And a town or family that fights will soon destroy itself.
text: Yeezuu k'a hɔ kʊ ŋ n'a hɔɔn dam ma bɩ dɔ bɩ, a a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «Kʊ k'a gʊɔɔ bɩr kʊ mam bɩ y'a darɛ. Kʊnɔ gɛɛ, har kʊ kʊmarɛ ba ʊ bɩ y'a dam sɔ.
translated: Jesus said to them, "See, your name is written in heaven. Therefore rejoice, for the angels rejoice over you."

ref: He told them, “Go to Bethlehem and search carefully for the child. As soon as you find him, let me know. I also want to go and worship him.”
text: A n ŋ nyɔɔ Betelehɛm ʊ, an n'a hɩrɛ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «K'a ta a laaka da nyɩ bɩ mim ma mɩŋŋa, k'a nyɩ bɩ yɩ, k'a a wusigə a b'a hɩ mɔɔ m, kʊ m ta m aa da ʊ sɔ.»

ref: Then Jesus spoke sternly to the demon. It went out of the boy, and right then he was healed.
text: Yeezuu n həən si zinə bɩ ma, an bɔ nyɩ bɩ mɛ ʊ, nyɩ bɩ n laafɩɩ yɩ wakatɩ bɩ do ʊ.
translated: Jesus said to them, "If you have faith, even if you say to this mountain, 'Move from here to there,' it will move."

ref: “What do you think about the Messiah? Whose family will he come from?” They answered, “He will be a son of King David.”
text: «Awɔɔ y'a hɔɔn dam Krista ma lʊnlɔ? Nka nyɩ nɩ?» Ŋ n a hɩ m, ŋ ʊ: «*Davɩd Nyɩ m.»
translated: Where is the one called Christ? What is your opinion?

ref: This made the king so furious that he sent an army to kill those murderers and burn down their city.
text: Cir bɩ nɔ n ma ʊ, an sʊrdaarɔ nyɔɔ ŋn'aa gərzenno nɔɔn duro zɩnzɛ, ŋnɩ sɛ da ŋ kʊ bɩ ma.
translated: My name is not Cir, and when the person was old for many years, he said he would do it.

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
text: Hɩrɔɔdɩ k'a b'a dɔ a ʊ, hɔdonno wɔɔ a yamba a m bɩ, a wuti nɔmaa gandaara m. A n gʊɔɔ nyɔɔ Betelehɛm ʊ kan kʊrɔ vantɔɔ k'a a so ŋ ʊ rɔ wɔɔ kɩ ʊ, kʊ ŋ n'aa nyɩnɔ poorero yaarɔ kʊ ŋ dɔɔraa hɩɩya gɛɛ ŋ n a ku rɔ wɔɔ zɩnzɛ. Amba hɔdonno wɔɔ a hɩ m, nyɩnta k'a ʊ, an laaka da k'a nyɩ bɩ yɩrɛ dɔmɩm dɔ bɩ ma.
translated: When Herod heard this, he was troubled, and all Jerusalem with him. He summoned the wise men and asked them carefully about the time the star appeared. Then he sent them to Bethlehem, saying, "Go and search carefully for the child, and when you find him, bring me word so that I may go and worship him too." But the wise men, having heard the king, went their way; and behold, the star which they had seen in the east went before them, until it came and stood over where the child was.

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: K'a dɩga bɛnnɔ ma: ŋ bɩr hɔ duure y, bɩ ŋ bɩr hɔ darɛ y, ŋ bɩr hɔ zarm sɩ ʊ y, b'awɔɔ Zɩ k'a nɩ arzana ʊ bɩ yɩ ŋ tʊrm! Awɔɔ n lɛ da ŋ nɩ gallɛm hɔka ra?
translated: My message says: I carry in my heart, I carry in my chest, I walk on your path, Zɩ declares the truth in your eyes! When will the time come?

ref: Some Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses said, “Teacher, we want you to show us a sign from heaven.”
text: Naa kur bɩ, wosocikarɩnsaambɔɔ kan farizɛɛnrɔ kɩ a sɩŋŋɩda ba, ŋn'a hɩ Yeezuu m, ŋ ʊ: «Karɩnsaamba, wɔɔ lɛ taa k'ɩ hoser ba k'ʊ a yɩ.»

ref: Servants are fortunate if their master comes and finds them doing their job.
text: Zibəənyɩ bɩ do nyɩntam mimbirenyɩntazaa, k'a lɛɛzaa b'a wusigə an bʊr har ʊ, an b'a ku an nɩ zibəə k'a ka ʊ bɩ barɛ.
translated: The seeds are sown with words of instruction, they sprout something visible on the road, and they harvest the seeds again to replant them.

ref: Others said, “Wait! Let's see if Elijah will come and save him.”
text: Bɩ, a muno wɔɔ y'a hɩrɛ, ŋ ʊ: «Jɩm cɩna, k'ʊ dɩga a Elii zɛm b'a bʊmbɔ gɛ?»
translated: Well, I heard something happened, so: Did Jim see where Elijah went to his village?

ref: I brought him to your disciples, but none of them could heal him.”
text: Mɔɔ n bʊr m ɩ karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ zi, bɩ, ŋ n a da ma, ŋn'a waa y.»

text: Bala, ɩbɩɩ meerbaa n n'ɩ bʊmbɔrɛ, ɩ meerbaa n n'ɩ dam mim ʊ sɔ.» Gʊɔɔ ɩ hoser yɛrɛ (Markɩ
translated: Watch therefore, for you do not know when the master of the house is coming, whether in the evening, or at midnight, or at cockcrow, or in the morning.

ref: A good tree cannot produce bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot produce good fruit.

translated: And the child is born when the animal is born, and the animal is born when the child is born.

ref: But while everyone was sleeping, an enemy came and scattered weed seeds in the field and then left.
text: Gum k'a bɩ da gʊɔɔ nɩ hinceem bam bɩ, gʊaa bɩ jɩm deem n bʊr, an bɩ sʊr duu hɔyaa bɩ barla, an a hʊr.

text: A m bɩ, Yeezuu n ŋ to b'ʊ, an a hʊr an bɔ kʊnɔ ʊ b'ʊ, an ta Betanii ʊ dɔ n mɛ m b'ʊ. Yeezuu lɛ kʊsɩ da sɔɔn ʊ (Markɩ

translated: The farmer sows the seed, and the seed becomes a plant. The plant grows, and it becomes a tree, and it bears fruit.

ref: Jesus used stories when he spoke to the people. In fact, he did not tell them anything without using stories.
text: Minno nɔɔn duro haay hɩ Yeezuu n nawʊrɛ ŋ nɩ hɔbɔkʊrɛnoom m. A bɩr hɔsɩ hɩm gʊɔɔ cir bɩ m, k'a hɩ bɛɛ a nawʊm hɔbɔkʊrɛnoom nɩ y.
translated: When Jesus saw the crowd, he went up on the mountain and sat down. His disciples came to him, and he began to teach them.

ref: But if you reject me, I will tell my Father in heaven you don't belong to me. Not Peace, but Trouble (Luke
text: Bɩ, kʊ gʊaa bɩsɩ ka mɔɔ ma gʊɔɔ lɛɛ, mɔɔ bɩsɩ karɛ a zaa ma sɔ mɔɔ Zɩ k'a nɩ arzana ʊ bɩ lɛɛ. Laafɩɩ bɛɛ bʊr y, yarbarɛ m (Likki
translated: Yes, when you go to the market, you will find the market full, but when you go to the farm, you will find the farm empty. The people are in the market, the market is full (Luke)

ref: Everything they do is just to show off in front of others. They even make a big show of wearing Scripture verses on their foreheads and arms, and they wear big tassels for everyone to see.
text: Ziro kʊ ŋ nɩ ŋ bam haay rɔ wɔɔ ba ŋ nawʊm kʊ gʊɔɔ m dɩga a ma, bɩ y'a ka, Wosoci lemim kʊ ŋ tɩr a kʊsɩ a tusə ma gɛɛ a za ma bɩ ba ŋ tɩr a ʊ jintə, ŋ tɩr pɛɛra lensəzannɔ dɔ a huuro rɛ, k'an bɩr yɩm.
translated: Ziro went to where the people were gathered and spoke to them, but they did not listen, so Wosoci's children taught them to learn and take it upon themselves, yet they still did not understand, so he helped them year after year until they finally left.

ref: So they went. At noon and again about three in the afternoon he returned to the market. And each time he made the same agreement with others who were loafing around with nothing to do.

text: Ŋ n a bɔ a zɩ b'ʊ kan kɔɔlʊʊ bɩ kɩ lɛɛm, ŋn'ɩ zure a jɛ ʊ. Yeezuu kan gʊɔɔ ciiro kɩ
translated: He came to the village to speak to the people, and Jesus went to another village.

text: A a da Galilee kʊrɔ wɔɔ zi haay, an nɩ hɔ dɩndarɛ ŋ nɩ ŋ sokʊmacɛrɔ wɔɔ ʊ, an nɩ lam zinəro ra. Yeezuu laafɩɩ ka kusimə ʊ (Matiyee
translated: He came from Galilee to the Jordan to be baptized by John, and to fulfill all righteousness. Jesus then said to him (Matthew)

ref: The guards shook from fear and fell down, as though they were dead.

translated: Surdaaro ate the dog, I am not well, my children are they, I gave them money.

ref: I promise that when any two of you on earth agree about something you are praying for, my Father in heaven will do it for you.
text: Mɔɔ m ya, m n'a hɩrɛ awɔɔ m sɔ: K'awɔɔ gʊaa hɩɩya a zɛ kʊ ra tara burə ʊ naa ʊ, k'a hɔ niŋŋə haay yɛ yaadarɛ nɔ ʊ, mɔɔ Zɩ k'a nɩ arzana ʊ bɩ hɔ bɩ do karɛ ŋ ʊ.
translated: My child, I am telling you: Come and see the way of the world and the people who are lost there; my child, the Lord has prepared good works for you to do.

ref: But Jesus knew what was in their minds, and he said, “Why are you thinking such evil things?
text: Yeezuu k'a hɔ k'a nɩ ŋ heer ʊ bɩ dɔ bɩ, a a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «Bɔ minto, awɔɔ n'a hɔɔn darɛ ncɩnaaʊ?
translated: Jesus looked at them and said, "Which is easier: to say 'Your sins are forgiven' or to say 'Rise up and walk'?"

ref: All this time Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were sitting across from the tomb.
text: Magdalaa kʊ Maarii kan Maarii vanta bɩ k'ɩ nyɩntam b'ʊ, ŋn'a meer nya yaa bɩ rɛ. N yɩ dɔm yaa bɩ ma
translated: Magdalene went to Mary because Mary had sent for her, and she came to her. And she said to her

text: A ba awɔɔ nawʊm ncɩnaaʊ an nɩ Woso lemim bɩ zaar bam a yɩnnɩbayɩrɔ wɔɔ m. Ziro ncɩnaaʊ rɔ wɔɔ lɔɔrɔ awɔɔ nɩ ŋ bam gʊta.» Hɔ k'a nɩ dintim kam gʊaa ma (Matiyee
translated: The kingdom of heaven is like a mustard seed that a man took and sowed in his field. Though it is the smallest of all seeds, when it grows up, it surpasses all herbs...

ref: If you greet only your friends, what's so great about this? Don't even unbelievers do that?
text: Bɩ, k'awɔɔ ɩ yaa darɛ a danyɩnɔ m bala, bɔ awɔɔ n'a bam, an nɩ yɩrɛ a hɔ? Kɩfɩɩrɔ wɔɔ bɩr a barɛ ncɩnaaʊ sɔ ra?
translated: What did you do to my child, and what did you do to your father, and what did you say to me? Why did you come to my house now?

ref: But they could not find any, even though many did come and tell lies. At last, two men came forward
text: Bɩ, ŋ bɩr mim yɩrɛ y, baa kʊ gʊɔɔ bʊr gʊta ŋnɩ ŋʊaar ba k'a ka ra bɩ, ŋ n a da ma, ŋnɩ hɔsɩ yɩ k'a ka ra y. Gʊɔɔ hɩɩyɔɔ ɩ bɔkarɛ ŋnɩ bʊr.
translated: Listen, I tell you the truth, when you come to work for many children, they will know when you do it. I do not want to give you the way you will do it. Go in peace and love their children.

ref: When the young man heard this, he went away sad, because he was very rich.
text: Bɩ, nyukumbueere bɩ k'a naa ma bɩ, a heer a zar gʊta, an a hʊr kan heerzar kɩ, bala, arzakazaa gʊta m.

ref: But none of you should be called a teacher. You have only one teacher, and all of you are like brothers and sisters.
text: B'awɔɔ, a bɩ yɛ kʊ n n'a bir karɩnsaamba y, bala, danyɩnɔ n'awɔɔ m haay, awɔɔ karɩnsaamba bɩ deem.
translated: B'awɔɔ said to them, "Go to Karinsaamba's house," and they came, and Karinsaamba did it.
